idea: Build CapacityLab, a manufacturing capacity scenario lab where planners adjust yield, downtime, available hours, and shifts, save two in-memory scenarios, and compare throughput visually and numerically.
audience: Production planning managers evaluating short-term capacity scenarios
outcome: Planners can see exactly how operating assumptions change throughput and compare two alternatives without spreadsheet ambiguity.
constraints: Local-only with fictional deterministic data and no backend, auth, database, analytics, APIs, or network calls
Document the throughput formula, units, validation bounds, and rounding
Charts need accessible text alternatives and numeric deltas
Responsive and keyboard accessible with visible focus
must_have_capabilities: Edit and validate yield, downtime, hours, shifts, and nominal rate
Calculate throughput using a documented deterministic formula
Save exactly two independent in-memory scenarios
Compare them in an accessible chart and numeric delta summary
Reset all assumptions and saved scenarios
visual_direction: A sophisticated but uncluttered scenario lab with aligned inputs, accessible charts, strong number formatting, and clear comparison states.
demo_preferences: Show the baseline, change yield, downtime, and shifts, save the alternative, compare both scenarios, show chart plus numeric deltas, then reset.
